overview.description: The Blueprint does not itself achieve any particular Essential Eight Maturity levels, but rather assists organisations in designing and building systems to achieve their desired maturity level based on their own operating context.
overview.minimum_requirement: Under the PSPF, government organisations must implement Maturity Level Two for each Essential Eight component.
overview.security_platform: Microsoft Defender XDR (Defender for Endpoint, Defender for Office 365, Defender for Identity, Defender for Cloud Apps, Defender Vulnerability Management, Entra ID Protection, Purview DLP)

patch_applications.name: Patch Applications
patch_applications.description: Manage and deploy patches for applications to mitigate known vulnerabilities
m365_technologies: Microsoft Intune (patch deployment for Windows endpoints)

asset_discovery.requirement: Automated asset discovery performed at least fortnightly

vulnerability_scanning.core_apps_defined_as: Office productivity suites, web browsers and extensions, email clients, PDF software, security products

unsupported_applications.requirement: Monitor vendor support lifecycles; remove unsupported applications prior to end-of-support
unsupported_applications.applies_to: Office suites, browsers, extensions, email clients, PDF software, Flash, security products

implementation_details.windows_endpoints: Microsoft Intune manages patches; Microsoft Defender for Endpoint applies updates

patch_operating_systems.name: Patch Operating Systems
patch_operating_systems.description: Manage and deploy patches for operating systems to mitigate known vulnerabilities
m365_technologies: Microsoft Intune (OS patch deployment for Windows endpoints)

asset_discovery.requirement: Automated method of asset discovery used at least fortnightly

unsupported_os.ML3_additional: Must use latest or previous OS release

implementation_details.windows_endpoints: Patches managed and deployed using Microsoft Intune, applied using Microsoft Defender for Endpoint
implementation_details.hybrid_servers: Alternative deployment mechanisms documented in organizational patch management processes

multi_factor_authentication.name: Multi-Factor Authentication
multi_factor_authentication.description: Implement MFA for access to systems and data, particularly for privileged access

ML2_ML3.prohibited: All password-based combinations and legacy authentication
ML2_ML3.note: Microsoft Authenticator is NOT phishing resistant

key_policies: Enforce phishing-resistant MFA for all users
Enforce phishing-resistant MFA for admin access
Enforce phishing-resistant MFA for Intune device enrollment
Block legacy authentication methods lacking MFA support
Block high-risk user accounts and compromised identities
Restrict access to iOS and Windows devices only
Require Intune device compliance across all devices
Web-based admin sessions expire after 4 hours
Standard user sessions expire after 12 hours

logging_requirements.ML2_ML3: All successful and unsuccessful MFA events must be captured and centrally logged to Microsoft Log Analytics
logging_requirements.log_protection: Event logs protected from unauthorized modification and deletion

phishing_resistance_strategy: Require managed devices via Conditional Access
Geographic restrictions using device GPS signals
FIDO2 keys as preferred phishing-resistant method

restrict_administrative_privileges.name: Restrict Administrative Privileges
restrict_administrative_privileges.description: Restrict and control administrative privileges to reduce the attack surface

privileged_access_validation.ML1_ML3: All access validated when first requested

all_levels: Privileged accounts cannot access unprivileged environments
Unprivileged accounts cannot access privileged environments
ML2_ML3: Privileged environments not virtualized within unprivileged ones
Administrative activities conducted through jump servers
operating_environment_separation.ML3: Administrative activities performed on Secure Admin Workstations (SAWs)

privileged_account_restrictions: Restricted from internet, email, and web service access
Access limited to required Microsoft management portals
assessment_checklist: Credential length and complexity standards (30+ characters)
Centralized identity management via Entra ID
RBAC implementation with principle of least privilege
Credential Guard and LSA protection enabled
Jump server or Secure Admin Workstation deployment (ML2+)

application_control.name: Application Control
application_control.description: Control which applications are allowed to execute on systems

wdac_configuration.script_enforcement: Enabled (forces PowerShell into constrained language mode)
wdac_configuration.managed_installer: Enabled (auto-allows apps deployed via Config Manager)
wdac_configuration.rule_methodology: Combination of publisher certificate and path rules
wdac_configuration.boot_config: Advanced Boot Options Menu disabled, Boot Audit on Failure enabled

application_control.validation_requirement: ML2 and ML3 require application control rule-sets validated on annual basis minimum

logging.ML2_ML3: All allowed and blocked application control events centrally logged from workstations, internet-facing servers, non-internet-facing servers

additional_controls.applocker_blocklists: Prevent administrators from accessing browsers and email clients

restrict_microsoft_office_macros.name: Restrict Microsoft Office Macros
restrict_microsoft_office_macros.description: Restrict and control Microsoft Office macro execution
m365_technologies: Microsoft 365 Apps Group Policy / Intune configuration profiles
requirements_all_levels: Disable all Microsoft Office macros unless demonstrated business requirement exists
Enable antivirus scanning for Office macros
Prevent user modification of macro security settings
requirements_ML2_ML3: Block Win32 API calls from macros (via ASR rules) for users with documented business needs
requirements_ML3: Macros execute only when: sandboxed, from Trusted Locations, or digitally signed by trusted publishers
Block enablement via Message Bar/Backstage for untrusted or non-V3 signatures

implementation_details.macro_execution: Restricted to trusted digital certificate signatures per ASD guidance
implementation_details.internet_origin_blocking: Internet-origin macros blocked; standard users cannot modify settings
implementation_details.antivirus_scanning: Microsoft Defender Antivirus and Defender for Endpoint provide scanning via Antimalware Scan Interface (AMSI) for runtime macro inspection
implementation_details.asr_rules: Attack Surface Reduction rules prevent Win32 API calls from macros
implementation_details.edr_logging: Defender for Endpoint centrally stores EDR logs including macro-enabled document execution

user_application_hardening.name: User Application Hardening
user_application_hardening.description: Harden user-facing applications to reduce the attack surface
requirements_all_levels: Web browsers with locked security settings, no Java/ad processing
Web browsers hardened using ASD and vendor hardening guidance
requirements_ML2_ML3: Office blocked from creating child processes
Office blocked from creating executable content
Office blocked from code injection into other processes
Microsoft Office configured to prevent activation of OLE packages
PDF software blocked from creating child processes
requirements_ML3: PowerShell configured for Constrained Language Mode

implementation_details.web_browser: Microsoft Edge hardened via WDAC, Java disabled, ad blocking via WDAC and gateway systems
implementation_details.office_suite: Child process creation blocked, executable content creation blocked, code injection blocked, OLE package activation prevented
implementation_details.powershell: Constrained Language Mode enforced via WDAC script enforcement

regular_backups.name: Regular Backups
regular_backups.description: Perform regular backups to enable recovery from ransomware and destructive attacks
backup_requirements_all_levels: Backups performed and retained according to business criticality
Backups synchronized to enable restoration to common point in time
Backups stored securely in a resilient manner
Backups tested regularly through disaster recovery exercises

native_m365_retention.onedrive: Recycle bin recovery up to 93 days
native_m365_retention.sharepoint_online: Recycle bin recovery up to 93 days
native_m365_retention.teams: Data retained indefinitely unless retention policies implemented

defender_xdr.rbac: Unified RBAC model supporting least privilege
defender_xdr.api_integration: SIEM-SOAR tools, ticketing systems, third-party apps

privilege_management.uac: All admins in Admin Approval Mode

windows_hardening.asr_rules: Multiple ASR rules enabled against credential theft, Office macro abuse, suspicious script execution

authentication_hardening.ntlm: NTLMv2 required; LM and NTLM refused

entra_id_password_policy.complexity: Three of four: uppercase, lowercase, numbers, symbols

key_requirements: Patch applications: 2-week SLA for critical core apps, weekly vulnerability scanning
Patch OS: 1-month SLA for critical workstation patches
MFA: Microsoft Authenticator (Passwordless) or Hardware Token; no SMS/email
Admin privileges: Access validated on request; privileged/unprivileged separation
Application control: WDAC on user profiles and temp folders; 7 filetypes controlled
Office macros: Disabled unless business need; block internet-origin; AV scanning
User app hardening: IE11 disabled; browser security locked; no Java/ads
Backups: Performed per business criticality; unprivileged users cannot access/modify others' backups

ML2.description: Target maturity for Australian Government (PSPF minimum)
key_requirements: Patch applications: 2-week SLA for critical core apps; fortnightly scanning for all apps
Patch OS: 48-hour SLA for internet-facing critical; 2-week non-critical
MFA: Phishing-resistant only (FIDO2, WHfB); all MFA events logged centrally
Admin privileges: 12-month revalidation; 45-day inactivity disable; jump servers; 30-char credentials
Application control: All locations controlled; Microsoft recommended blocklist; annual rule validation; all events logged
Office macros: Win32 API calls blocked via ASR rules
User app hardening: Office child process/executable/injection blocked; OLE blocked; PDF child process blocked
Backups: Privileged non-backup-admin access restricted; cannot modify/delete
Patch applications: 48-hour SLA for ALL critical patches
Patch OS: 48-hour SLA for critical workstation patches; latest/previous release only
MFA: Phishing-resistant; real-time analysis for all systems including workstations
Admin privileges: Secure Admin Workstations; Credential Guard; LSA protection; memory integrity
Application control: Non-internet-facing servers included; drivers controlled; vulnerable driver blocklist
Office macros: Trusted Locations/publishers/sandboxed only; pre-signature malicious code checking
User app hardening: .NET 3.5 removed; PS 2.0 removed; PS Constrained Language Mode
Backups: Backup admins cannot modify/delete during retention; users cannot access own backups
